report_name: NAIC Confirms Breach via Critical Oracle PeopleSoft Zero-Day Flaw Exploited by ShinyHunters

threat_actors.name: ShinyHunters
threat_actors.description: A data extortion group that exploited the Oracle PeopleSoft zero-day vulnerability CVE-2026-35273 to breach the National Association of Insurance Commissioners (NAIC) and over 100 other organizations. The group steals data and threatens to leak it unless a ransom is paid.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: ShinyHunters exploited the zero-day vulnerability CVE-2026-35273 in the internet-facing Oracle PeopleSoft application to gain initial access and achieve unauthenticated remote code execution.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: As a data extortion group, ShinyHunters exfiltrates compromised data from victim networks to their own infrastructure to support their extortion activities.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-35273
vulnerabilities.description: A critical remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability in Oracle PeopleSoft Enterprise PeopleTools that allows an unauthenticated attacker with network access via HTTP to completely compromise the system. The flaw was exploited as a zero-day by ShinyHunters.

identities.name: National Association of Insurance Commissioners (NAIC)
identities.description: A U.S. association of insurance regulators that was breached by ShinyHunters via the exploitation of CVE-2026-35273 in their Oracle PeopleSoft system.

identities.name: Higher education sector
identities.description: The primary target sector of the ShinyHunters campaign exploiting CVE-2026-35273, with over 100 organizations affected.

indicators.name: Suspicious Child Process of Oracle PeopleSoft Server
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Child Process of Oracle PeopleSoft Server
id: 2b1a9c8d-4e5f-4a3b-9c8d-1e2f3a4b5c6d
status: experimental
description: Detects the Oracle PeopleSoft application server process (java.exe or tuxedo.exe) spawning a command shell, which may indicate remote code execution via a vulnerability like CVE-2026-35273.
references:
  - https://arcticwolf.com/resources/blog/critical-oracle-peoplesoft-vulnerability-actively-exploited-in-shinyhunters-campaign/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-24
tags:
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.t1059
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\java.exe'
      - '\tuxedo.exe'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
  condition: all of selection_*
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative scripts or application functions that spawn shells from the application server process.
indicators.description: Detects the Oracle PeopleSoft application server process spawning a command shell (cmd.exe, powershell.exe), which is a strong indicator of remote code execution, as described in the hunting hints for CVE-2026-35273.

relationships.source_name: Suspicious Child Process of Oracle PeopleSoft Server
Suspicious Child Process of Oracle PeopleSoft Server
